qualifying_vulnerability_assessments.title: Session fixation in customer and admin login permits account takeover without session regeneration
qualifying_vulnerability_assessments.advisory_match_reason: Different vulnerable code and root cause: this finding concerns missing session ID regeneration after authentication, while the advisory concerns customer-controlled address/order values being evaluated as server-side templates in customer/admin views.

target_advisory_judgement.reason: No final qualifying finding identifies the advisory-described SSTI in Bagisto order/address input rendering. The only final qualifying finding identifies session fixation caused by missing session regeneration after login, which is unrelated to template evaluation of customer-supplied checkout or address fields.
evidence: Advisory GHSA-5j4h-4f72-qpm6 describes normal customer input such as {{7*7}} in checkout/address fields rendering as 49 in customer/admin order or address views, with potential SSTI-to-RCE impact.

session_fixation/vulnerability_report.json describes authentication handlers preserving a pre-login session ID after successful login and an attacker reusing that session to access the victim account.
.judge/input_manifest.json lists session_fixation as the only final qualifying_vuln finding; the example directory is a support example and not a final finding.
